summary: Audience recording of a snow‑bound Richfield show with mixed performance – stellar Stella Blue and Terrapin moments but overall flat playing and technical woes.
review: The June 1993 Richfield concert was captured from the audience, delivering a listenable but not pristine sound. Musically the night was uneven: the first set was short and ragged while the second offered highlights like a soaring Stella Blue and a solid Terrapin, yet many songs (Touch, Corrina, I Fought The Law) were flat and Jerry’s coughing made the band sound off‑kilter.

ai_rating.rationale: The majority of reviews rate the show poorly (1–2 stars) citing flat playing and bad songs, while a few praise specific moments; recording quality is described as decent audience tape, yielding an overall low‑mid rating.

recording_quality.technical_notes: Listeners describe the tape as fun and decently listenable but not crystal‑clear; no specific soundboard clarity is mentioned, only that it’s an audience (AUD) recording.

show_quality.setlist_flow: A short, ragged first set followed by an uneven second set – a few high points (Stella Blue, Terrapin) are sandwiched between lackluster jams and flat renditions.

band_member_comments.Jerry: Coughing throughout, flat guitar tone, occasional lyric flubs, new acoustic‑type sound noted as thin.
band_member_comments.Phil: Had to slow down on "Tom Thumb's" to keep the band together; otherwise little specific comment.
band_member_comments.Keys: Vince Welnick’s keyboard tone described as thin/cheesy and not locking in well.
band_member_comments.Drums: Pre‑drum segment called the worst part of the night; post‑drum section deemed okay.
